issues.body: The record says private inputs are shared t-of-n with t=floor((n-1)/2), and later defines F_share as distributing a t-of-n sharing. But the same document uses t as the maximum corrupt coalition / polynomial degree (any t shares reveal nothing, n=2t+1). Such a Shamir sharing reconstructs from t+1 shares, not t. This conflates the privacy threshold with the reconstruction threshold in the load-bearing per-stage ideal functionality and can invalidate subsequent threshold reasoning.
issues.fix_hint: Replace both t-of-n descriptions with unambiguous degree-t Shamir terminology, explicitly stating that any t shares reveal no information and t+1 valid shares reconstruct, with t=floor((n-1)/2). Check nearby uses of threshold open/reconstruct for the same convention.
issues.title: Shamir reconstruction threshold is misstated as t-of-n

verdict.summary: The UC/composition caveats remain appropriately conditional and preserve the external-audit and semi-honest limitations, but the new posture record materially misstates the Shamir access structure as t-of-n while simultaneously defining t as the corruption/privacy threshold. In the stated degree-t scheme, reconstruction requires t+1 shares. Because this error appears in the ideal functionality for the sharing stage of a security-model document, the change is not yet correct as scoped. Round 3 reported no findings, so this newly identified security-model error makes round 4 regressing.
